sections.title: THESIS
sections.subtitle: Procedural Narrative Systems in Open-World Games: A Constraint-Based Approach to Emergent Storytelling

sections.title: Procedural Narrative Systems in Open-World Games
sections.subtitle: A Constraint-Based Approach to Emergent Storytelling
sections.abstract: Modern open-world games generate vast spatial environments procedurally, yet their narratives remain hand-authored — a fundamental mismatch that limits the depth of player agency. This thesis proposes a constraint-based model for procedural narrative generation that preserves authorial intent while enabling combinatorial story diversity.

contributions.label: Formal Model
contributions.desc: A constraint propagation model for narrative event graphs with proof of convergence under reasonable assumptions.

contributions.label: Empirical Study
contributions.desc: First controlled study comparing player perception of authored vs. constraint-generated narrative in an interactive context.

contributions.label: Design Patterns
contributions.desc: 5 actionable design patterns for implementing procedural narrative in commercial game development workflows.

contributions.label: Open Prototype
contributions.desc: A Unity-integrated prototype system demonstrating the model in a playable environment, published open-source.

chapters.title: The Problem of Authored Emergence
chapters.summary: Traditional narrative design in games operates on a branching tree model. This chapter establishes the tension between author intent and systemic emergence as the central problem of procedural narrative design.

chapters.title: Grammars for Story: Rule-Based Narrative Systems
chapters.summary: Examining existing computational approaches to narrative generation — from classic story grammars to modern drama managers.

chapters.title: Constraint Propagation as Narrative Curation
chapters.summary: A formal model for narrative constraint propagation demonstrated with a working prototype.

chapters.title: Empirical Study: Player Perception of Authored vs. Generated Narrative
chapters.summary: Results from a 120-participant study. Participants could not reliably distinguish generated from authored sequences above chance.

chapters.title: Toward a Practice: Design Patterns for Procedural Narrative
chapters.summary: 5 actionable design patterns for game developers: the Narrative Seed, Constraint Stack, Dramatic Arc Enforcer, and Memory-Weighted Event Selector.

sections.result: Best Technical Achievement — DigiPen Senior Capstone 2020
